skills.name: translator
skills.description: Translate text between Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, French, Spanish and more via free APIs.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: translator
description: Translate text between languages using free APIs
---

# Translator

Translate text between languages. Try APIs in order until one works.

## Method 1 — MyMemory (free, no auth)
```bash
curl -s "https://api.mymemory.translated.net/get?q=<ENCODED_TEXT>&langpair=<SRC>|<TGT>" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['responseData']['translatedText'])"
```
Example — translate "Hello" from English to Chinese:
```bash
curl -s "https://api.mymemory.translated.net/get?q=Hello+world&langpair=en|zh" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['responseData']['translatedText'])"
```

## Method 2 — web_search fallback
Use web_search with query: `translate "<text>" to <language>`

## Language codes
`en` English · `zh` Chinese · `ja` Japanese · `ko` Korean · `fr` French · `de` German · `es` Spanish · `ru` Russian · `ar` Arabic · `pt` Portuguese

skills.name: pomodoro
skills.description: Run focused work sessions with the Pomodoro technique — 25 min work, 5 min break, tracked in terminal.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: pomodoro
description: Pomodoro focus timer using bash sleep
---

# Pomodoro Timer

Run timed Pomodoro work/break sessions via bash.

## Start a 25-minute work session (background)
```bash
(
  sleep 1500
  echo "DONE" > /tmp/pomodoro_status
  echo "🍅 Pomodoro complete! Time for a break."
) &
echo $! > /tmp/pomodoro.pid
echo "Pomodoro started (PID $(cat /tmp/pomodoro.pid)). 25 minutes remaining."
```

## Check status
```bash
if [ -f /tmp/pomodoro_status ]; then
  echo "✅ Pomodoro complete!"
else
  PID=$(cat /tmp/pomodoro.pid 2>/dev/null)
  if kill -0 $PID 2>/dev/null; then echo "⏳ Still running (PID $PID)"; else echo "Timer not running"; fi
fi
```

## Cancel
```bash
kill $(cat /tmp/pomodoro.pid 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; rm -f /tmp/pomodoro.pid /tmp/pomodoro_status; echo "Cancelled"
```

## Quick short timer (custom minutes)
```bash
MINS=5; sleep $((MINS*60)) && echo "⏰ ${MINS}min timer done"
```

skills.name: system-monitor
skills.description: Check CPU, memory, disk usage and running processes inside the Docker container.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: system-monitor
description: Monitor CPU, memory, disk and processes via bash
---

# System Monitor

Check system resource usage via bash commands inside the container.

## CPU & Memory summary
```bash
echo "=== CPU ==="; top -bn1 | grep -E "^(%Cpu|Cpu)" | head -3
echo "=== Memory ==="; free -h
echo "=== Load ==="; uptime
```

## Disk usage
```bash
df -h --output=source,size,used,avail,pcent,target | head -20
```

## Top processes by CPU
```bash
ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -15
```

## Top processes by memory
```bash
ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -10
```

## Network connections
```bash
ss -tuln 2>/dev/null || netstat -tuln 2>/dev/null
```

## All-in-one snapshot
```bash
echo "CPU:"; grep 'cpu ' /proc/stat | awk '{usage=($2+$4)*100/($2+$3+$4+$5)} END {print usage "%"}';
echo "MEM:"; free -h | grep Mem;
echo "DISK:"; df -h / | tail -1;
echo "PROCS:"; ps aux | wc -l
```

skills.name: ip-lookup
skills.description: Look up geolocation, ISP, and network info for any IP address or your own public IP.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: ip-lookup
description: Look up IP geolocation and network info
---

# IP Lookup

## Look up any IP address
```bash
curl -s "http://ip-api.com/json/<IP_ADDRESS>?fields=status,country,regionName,city,isp,org,as,query" \
  | python3 -m json.tool
```

## Get your own public IP
```bash
curl -s https://api.ipify.org
```

## Full details on your public IP
```bash
curl -s "http://ip-api.com/json/" | python3 -m json.tool
```

## Batch lookup (multiple IPs)
```bash
for IP in 8.8.8.8 1.1.1.1; do
  echo "--- $IP ---"
  curl -s "http://ip-api.com/json/$IP?fields=country,city,isp,query" | python3 -m json.tool
done
```

Replace `<IP_ADDRESS>` with the actual IP or hostname.

skills.name: uuid-gen
skills.description: Generate UUIDs (v4), random hex tokens, and secure passwords of any length.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: uuid-gen
description: Generate UUIDs, random tokens and secure passwords
---

# UUID & Random Generator

## Generate UUID v4
```bash
python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())"
```

## Generate multiple UUIDs
```bash
python3 -c "import uuid; [print(uuid.uuid4()) for _ in range(5)]"
```

## Secure random hex token (64 chars)
```bash
python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"
```

## URL-safe token
```bash
python3 -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))"
```

## Random password (letters + digits + symbols)
```bash
python3 -c "
import secrets, string
chars = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + '!@#\$%^&*'
print(''.join(secrets.choice(chars) for _ in range(20)))
"
```

## Bulk generation
```bash
python3 -c "import uuid, secrets; [print(f'{uuid.uuid4()} | {secrets.token_hex(16)}') for _ in range(3)]"
```

skills.name: json-tools
skills.description: Format, validate, compact, and query JSON data from files or stdin.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: json-tools
description: Format, validate and query JSON data via bash
---

# JSON Tools

## Pretty-print JSON string
```bash
echo '<JSON_STRING>' | python3 -m json.tool
```

## Pretty-print JSON file
```bash
python3 -m json.tool <FILE_PATH>
```

## Validate JSON file
```bash
python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(open('<FILE>')); print('✅ Valid JSON')" 2>&1
```

## Extract a field
```bash
echo '<JSON>' | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['<KEY>'])"
```

## Compact / minify JSON
```bash
python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(json.load(sys.stdin), ensure_ascii=False, separators=(',',':')))"
```

## Sort keys
```bash
python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.dumps(json.load(sys.stdin), sort_keys=True, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))"
```

## Count items in array
```bash
echo '<JSON_ARRAY>' | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin)))"
```

skills.name: git-assistant
skills.description: Common git workflows: status overview, diff, branch management, log summary, and commit helpers.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: git-assistant
description: Git workflow helper via bash
---

# Git Assistant

Helper for common git operations. Use `ls /host-home` to find repo paths first.

## Quick status overview
```bash
REPO=<REPO_PATH>
git -C $REPO status --short && echo "---" && git -C $REPO log --oneline -5
```

## View uncommitted changes
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> diff --stat HEAD
```

## Full diff of changes
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> diff HEAD
```

## Commit all changes
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> add -A && git -C <REPO_PATH> commit -m "<message>"
```

## Create and switch to new branch
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> checkout -b <branch-name>
```

## List branches
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> branch -av
```

## Pretty commit log
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> log --oneline --graph --decorate -20
```

## Search commits by keyword
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> log --oneline --grep="<keyword>" -20
```

## Show files changed in last commit
```bash
git -C <REPO_PATH> show --stat HEAD
```

skills.name: docker-helper
skills.description: Manage Docker containers, images, and volumes on the host machine via the Docker socket.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: docker-helper
description: Manage Docker containers and images via bash
---

# Docker Helper

Run Docker commands via bash. The container has access to the host Docker socket at `/var/run/docker.sock`.

## List running containers
```bash
docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"
```

## List all containers
```bash
docker ps -a --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Image}}"
```

## View container logs
```bash
docker logs <container_name> --tail=50 -f
```

## Restart a container
```bash
docker restart <container_name>
```

## Execute command in container
```bash
docker exec -it <container_name> <command>
```

## List images
```bash
docker images --format "table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.Size}}"
```

## Disk usage overview
```bash
docker system df
```

## Docker Compose — restart a service
```bash
cd <compose_dir> && docker compose restart <service_name>
```

## Docker Compose — view all service logs
```bash
cd <compose_dir> && docker compose logs --tail=50
```

skills.name: countdown
skills.description: Show a live countdown in terminal for any number of minutes or seconds.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: countdown
description: Live countdown timer via bash
---

# Countdown Timer

## Countdown with live display (seconds)
```bash
for i in $(seq <SECONDS> -1 1); do
  printf "\r⏳ %02d:%02d remaining..." $((i/60)) $((i%60))
  sleep 1
done
echo -e "\n⏰ Time's up!"
```

## Quick N-minute timer
```bash
MINS=10
for i in $(seq $((MINS*60)) -1 1); do
  printf "\r⏳ %02d:%02d" $((i/60)) $((i%60))
  sleep 1
done
echo -e "\n⏰ Done!"
```

## Silent background countdown (check later)
```bash
MINS=5
(
  sleep $((MINS*60))
  echo "DONE" > /tmp/countdown_done
) &
echo "Timer started: ${MINS}min. Check /tmp/countdown_done for completion."
```

## Check background timer
```bash
cat /tmp/countdown_done 2>/dev/null && echo "Complete!" || echo "Still running..."
```

skills.name: http-tester
skills.description: Test HTTP endpoints: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE requests with headers, body, timing, and response inspection.
skills.skill_md:
---
name: http-tester
description: Test HTTP endpoints with curl
---

# HTTP Tester

Test HTTP endpoints with detailed output. Always use `host.docker.internal` instead of `127.0.0.1` for local services.

## GET request
```bash
curl -v -s "<URL>" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || cat
```

## POST with JSON body
```bash
curl -s -X POST "<URL>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '<JSON_BODY>' \
  | python3 -m json.tool
```

## PUT request
```bash
curl -s -X PUT "<URL>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '<JSON_BODY>'
```

## DELETE request
```bash
curl -s -X DELETE "<URL>"
```

## With auth header
```bash
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>" "<URL>"
```

## Measure response time
```bash
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} | Time: %{time_total}s | Size: %{size_download} bytes" "<URL>"
```

## Test multiple endpoints
```bash
for URL in <URL1> <URL2>; do
  CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$URL")
  echo "$CODE $URL"
done
```

**Remember**: Use `http://host.docker.internal:<PORT>` for local services running on the host.
